items.title: Berkeley Lab to Lead 13 New Genesis Mission AI Projects
items.content_text: Researchers will apply AI, supercomputing, and advanced instruments to accelerate discoveries for energy, science, and national security.

items.title: U.S. Department of Energy invests in Fermilab projects to accelerate AI-enabled scientific discovery
items.content_text: The awards will support Fermilab projects by leveraging AI to boost innovation and technology development, advance DOE’s Genesis Mission and accelerate scientific discovery.

items.title: Fermilab installs first beamline component for new state-of-the-art accelerator
items.content_text: Fermilab crews have successfully placed the first beamline component in the tunnel that will house the powerful new PIP-II linear accelerator, marking a major milestone for the lab’s future neutrino research program.

items.title: Fermilab marks 70 years of neutrino science and leads next-generation experiment
items.content_text: As Fermilab commemorates 70 years since the discovery of the neutrino at the Savannah River Site in the United States, the lab also celebrates its multiple generations of pioneering neutrino research. Today, Fermilab is building the next era of neutrino discovery through the development of the world-leading Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment.

items.title: Fermilab’s Neutrino Physics Center hosts inaugural DUNE Data Analysis School
items.content_text: Fermilab hosted the inaugural DUNE Data Analysis School, organized through the lab’s Neutrino Physics Center. The school brought together early-career researchers for intensive training in the software and analysis tools needed to perform physics analyses for the upcoming Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment. The program marks a major step in preparing the scientific workforce for DUNE and is expected to become a recurring training initiative.

items.title: Action! NSF–DOE Vera C. Rubin Observatory begins capturing the greatest cosmic movie ever made
items.content_text: The 10-year Legacy Survey of Space and Time has officially started, marking the beginning of a new era in astronomy and astrophysics.

items.title: Fermilab’s Vishvas Pandey elected to co-lead international neutrino research collaboration
items.content_text: Pandey’s term as co-spokesperson of NuSTEC will focus on advancing higher levels of precision for the next generation of neutrino experiments, including the Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment, an international collaboration hosted by Fermilab.

items.title: Linda Cremonesi elected as co-spokesperson for NOvA neutrino experiment
items.content_text: Currently, NOvA is Fermilab’s leading long-baseline neutrino experiment. Cremonesi is now co-leading the international collaboration of scientists behind it.

items.title: Christina Wang of Fermilab receives prestigious award for advances in dark matter detection
items.content_text: Christina Wang, a postdoctoral researcher and Lederman Fellow at Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory, has been awarded the American Physical Society’s 2026 Mitsuyoshi Tanaka Award in Experimental Particle Physics for her pioneering work in particle detection. Her innovative techniques, which open new pathways for identifying elusive dark matter candidates, are advancing the search for physics beyond the Standard Model.

items.title: Fermilab storage infrastructure enables AI-driven scientific and research discovery for DOE’s Genesis Mission
items.content_text: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Genesis Mission is building a new era of AI-driven scientific discovery — and it requires far more than powerful supercomputers to succeed. To support this national effort, Fermilab’s Fermi Data Platform is providing secure, large-scale data infrastructure needed to make advanced AI research possible across the American Science Cloud.

items.content_text: The U.S. National Science Foundation today announced $83 million in awards through the Integrated Data Systems and Services (IDSS) program. The awards will expand access to data infrastructure…

items.content_text: The U.S. National Science Foundation announced it awarded the newest NSF Regional Innovation Engines (NSF Engines) awards to 12 U.S. teams across 20 states. These NSF Engines will build and scale…

items.content_text: The U.S. National Science Foundation today announced Project Triad, a first-of-its-kind initiative to integrate quantum sensing, quantum networking and quantum computing into a single operational...

items.content_text: U.S. National Science Foundation-supported professor Tao Zhou discusses the development of a new 3D-printed hydrogel-based bioelectronic device for hypertension treatment...

items.title: Discovery of a p-wave magnet in a metal
items.content_text: RIKEN researchers have found a metallic p-wave magnet that has a commensurate spin helix.

items.title: Theoretical predictions of unusual nonlinear thermoelectric effect confirmed
items.content_text: RIKEN researchers have observed the nonlinear chiral thermoelectric Hall effect in the semiconductor tellurium.

items.title: Uncovering the link between epigenetic modifications and chromatin structure
items.content_text: RIKEN researchers have found that histone acetylation directly controls chromatin architecture.

items.title: Long-overlooked gene for deglycosylating enzyme in fish embryo has been revealed
items.content_text: RIKEN researchers have identified the gene that encodes acid peptide: N-glycanase (PNGase) in zebrafish.

items.title: Quantum geometry lens shines new light on solids
items.content_text: RIKEN researchers have established bounds for various physical observables in solids by considering the quantum geometric tensor.

items.title: How energy is transferred in photosynthetic bacteria
items.content_text: RIKEN researchers have analyzed the structure and energy transfer of the phycobilisome–photosystem II megacomplex in a thermophilic cyanobacterium.

items.title: STEM Community Day 2026 | SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory
items.content_text: Learn about our science, people, facilities and partners. Discover our history and vision for the future.

items.title: Fusion energy | SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory
items.content_text: Harnessing nuclear fusion would bring the clean, unlimited energy of the stars to Earth.

items.title: LCLS-II High Energy Upgrade | SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory
items.content_text: Now, we’re turning up the energy. The high-energy upgrade to LCLS will extend its ultrafast megahertz capability into the “hard,” or high-energy, X-ray regime.

items.title: SLAC to lead Genesis Mission AI project to recover critical metals from lithium-ion battery waste
items.content_text: Researchers from the SLAC-Stanford Battery Center join USC in a project to help secure the U.S. supply of critical minerals.

items.title: Action! NSF–DOE Vera C. Rubin Observatory begins capturing the greatest cosmic movie ever made
items.content_text: The 10-year Legacy Survey of Space and Time has officially started, marking the beginning of a new era in astronomy and astrophysics.

items.title: SLAC researchers ‘watch’ molecules steered by laser light
items.content_text: Using the powerful LCLS X-ray laser, researchers at SLAC have directly imaged for the first time how molecules rearrange during a chemical reaction controlled by light.

items.title: SLAC’s premier facilities and record-setting datasets fuel DOE Genesis Mission
items.content_text: The lab’s contributions to the national AI initiative are bolstered by its breakthrough scientific tools, unprecedented data and unique partnerships that help illuminate nature from the expansion of the universe to the motions of electrons.

items.title: Building the quantum toolbox
items.content_text: As QuantISED enters its next phase in its effort to develop quantum technologies capable of exploring some of the universe’s biggest unanswered questions, scientists reflect on what the first round made possible and what comes next.

items.title: SLAC and Stanford researchers find an inexpensive way to make batteries last longer
items.content_text: By adjusting the heating process when making lithium-ion cathodes, the team created batteries that retained nearly 93% of their energy after 500 cycles.

items.title: Researchers pioneer method to rapidly design proteins
items.content_text: Their strategy revealed new details that open the door to designing proteins with powerful abilities that could ultimately benefit medicine and manufacturing.
